# Questions in Livestream 25

Live Stream #25: The Canonical Age For Levitical Priests

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:06</a> | Does he know of anywhere to study Syriac and the spread of Christianity along the Silk Road? | He called it a worthwhile topic but said it is outside his field and he could not responsibly recommend programs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=367" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:07</a> | Did Egyptians think the Milky Way was the heavenly Nile? | No. He said the celestial Nile was conceived differently, while the Milky Way was identified with the body of the sky goddess Nut. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=490" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:10</a> | What does he think of Titus Kennedy's work on evidence for the Exodus? | He was strongly negative and said Kennedy mishandles dates and archaeology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=634" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:34</a> | What are his thoughts on revisionist claims that the period around 1100-900 BC is overstretched? | He rejected the claim and said conventional chronology is better supported by synchronisms with Assyria, Egypt, and surrounding nations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=752" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:32</a> | Is Herod's slaughter of the infants a historical parallel to Pharaoh's killing of Hebrew sons, and should we expect physical evidence? | Yes, he saw it as an Exodus parallel, but not one likely to leave identifiable archaeological remains. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=988" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:28</a> | Did Egyptians have dragons, and if not, why not? | He said Egypt had dragon-like demon imagery, but crocodiles, cobras, and other river creatures filled many of the roles dragons fill elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=1188" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:48</a> | Does he agree that Deuteronomy's structure supports an early date because it resembles Hittite suzerainty treaties? | Yes. He said Kitchen's treaty-structure argument is complicated but basically sound. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=1492" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:52</a> | Is the name Potiphar anachronistic for Joseph's period? | No. He said it is a genuine Egyptian name type and fits the broader naming patterns well enough. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=1713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:33</a> | Did female virginity before marriage matter much in ancient Egypt? | Not in the way it did in ancient Israel. He described Egyptian sexual norms as comparatively permissive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=1851" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:51</a> | Did Israelites take eschatology and resurrection beliefs from Persia? | He said ancient religions shared and adapted ideas, so Israel could draw from surrounding cultures without that undermining inspiration or infallibility. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=2182" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:22</a> | Is there really a contradiction over Moses's father-in-law being called Jethro or Reuel? | He said no. Multiple names within tribal societies, or different family relationships across Moses's marriages, are enough to keep it from being a real contradiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=2390" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:50</a> | Was there a real Tower of Babel? | Yes, but he framed it as a real ziggurat-like place for man-centered worship rather than a fantastically tall tower reaching outer space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=2660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:20</a> | Should `raqia` be translated as dome instead of expanse or heaven? | He said "expanse" or "celestial ocean" are better, while "dome" and "heavens" are nearer the edges of the term's meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=2997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:57</a> | What are his thoughts on the Kregel Exegetical Library and Duane Garrett's Exodus commentary? | He had not read Garrett's commentary and warned that commentary quality varies book by book, so no series should be treated as uniformly best. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=3146" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:26</a> | Is Genesis 10:10-11 referring to the Uruk expansion? | He regarded that identification as possible but too weakly evidenced to adopt confidently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=3402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:42</a> | In what ways did Sumerian culture affect the writing of Genesis? | Very little directly by the time Genesis was written down, in his view, because Sumerian culture was already long gone except perhaps in distant landmarks and names. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=3678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:18</a> | How did snake charming work in the ancient Near East, and where did it come from? | He said it is attested across the Near East and seems to have worked broadly like later snake charming, with performance, handling, and family transmission of the craft. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=3929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:29</a> | Did ancient Near Eastern peoples have eschatologies, and do pre-exilic Hebrew texts show one? | Yes. He distinguished personal from final eschatology and said both appear in Hebrew texts, though final eschatology becomes clearer later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=4193" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:53</a> | What are his thoughts on the recently announced tomb of Ramesses II's treasurer? | He was waiting for publication, but said early reports and photos suggested a well-preserved tomb with potentially valuable evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=4297" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:37</a> | What is his preferred commentary on Exodus? | He said he does not have one preferred Exodus commentary, normally uses several, and might choose NICOT if forced to pick. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=4616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:56</a> | Is "Mystery Babylon" in Revelation using Ishtar-like imagery? | Partly, but he pushed the reading toward the wider idea of apostate religion rather than a simple goddess identification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=5001" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:21</a> | Has he personally had a supernatural encounter, and what can be classified as supernatural? | He said yes, he has seen spirit beings, but he was not ready to discuss the details. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=5104" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:04</a> | Is there Egyptian significance to the long ages in Genesis 5? | He noted Egyptian king lists give very long reigns to gods, but argued Genesis 5 is better understood as clan ages rather than individual lifespans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=5415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:15</a> | Which film best depicts ancient Egypt? | He did not name a definitive best film, but said Cecil B. DeMille's The Ten Commandments includes many surprisingly accurate Egyptian details despite imperfections. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=5530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:10</a> | Would Egyptian and other ancient idol worshipers have felt tension between divine superiority and human manipulation of the gods? | Yes, but differently by culture. He said Egyptians tried to please dangerous gods, while Canaanites more readily treated idols as leverage points for control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=6147" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:27</a> | Is Papyrus Anastasi I evidence for the Anakim or giant traditions? | He thought it gives some support, since its description of unusually tall Shasu helps explain why giant reports in Canaan could have had a real basis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=6361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:01</a> | Did Egypt have a concept similar to ghosts? | Yes. He said Egyptians expected regular interaction with the dead, including dream visitations and communication with ancestors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=6515" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:35</a> | If scripture contains numerical problems, how can it still be called infallible? | He limited infallibility to what scripture actually asserts and to the original writings, and said ancient number usage is often idiomatic or textually unstable rather than simply "error." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=7072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:52</a> | Does God implicitly condemn space exploration and settlement? | He said there is no sweeping biblical prohibition, though some theologians have argued from the earth/heavens distinction and practical settlement would be difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/au5AYPIFPbY?t=7327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:07</a> | Has he ever played the ancient Egyptian game Senet? | No, at least not by rules he would treat as ancient; the boards survive, but the original rulebook does not and modern rules are reconstructed guesses. |
